candidates.title: Alberta premier tells First Nations chiefs to 'check themselves' over treason accusation
candidates.description: The Assembly of Treaty Chiefs unanimously voted this week to ask RCMP to look into whether Alberta's fall referendum amounts to criminal treason by Premier Danielle Smith and her United Conservative Party.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: After top court ruling, Quebec to prioritize long-term residents for subsidized daycare
candidates.description: The Quebec government will be prioritizing families who "live and work in Quebec permanently" over asylum seekers for subsidized spots in daycares, according to the province's families minister.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: NHL fans will now have to pay to watch games on Sportsnet. What that means for Rogers — and CBC
candidates.description: The National Hockey League season came to a close this month and with it came the end of an era as longtime partners Rogers and CBC were unable to come to a new agreement for NHL broadcasts. So Canadian fans will only be able to watch games in Canada if they pay for a Sportsnet cable or streaming subscription.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Innu Nation accuses N.L. government of capping group’s history at 300 years — an 'erasure,' say Innu leaders
candidates.description: Innu Cultural Guardian Jodie Ashini says she thought National Indigenous Peoples Day, June 21, would be the perfect day to open the Innu Pakassiun exhibit at the Labrador Interpretation Centre in North West River.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Eligible doctors in Alberta can work in both public, private systems starting this fall
candidates.description: In order to be eligible for dual practice, doctors must do a minimum number of hours in the public system first. The province says those hour requirements along with other safeguards will protect the public system.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 19-year-old arrested in connection with U.S. Consulate shooting in Toronto
candidates.description: A 19-year-old has been arrested and charged in connection with a shooting at the U.S. consulate in March, said police.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'No plans right now' for grizzly hunting season in Alberta, minister says as rural residents call for hunt
candidates.description: Amid calls from rural municipalities for the first grizzly bear hunting season in two decades to be opened in Alberta, Forestry and Parks Minister Todd Loewen says it's not currently on the table. He says the province is focused on helping the threatened species' population recover through conservation and non-lethal wildlife management.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: An estimated 20,000 Alberta kids face waiting years to get therapy for disabilities
candidates.description: An estimated 20,000 children are stuck on Alberta’s Family Support for Children with Disabilities (FSCD) waiting list, facing years of delays to get speech, behavioural and other treatment or therapy for autism and various disabilities.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 60 years ago, Agent Orange was sprayed at CFB Gagetown. Vets want to know: are toxins still in the soil?
candidates.description: Sixty years after Agent Orange got sprayed at CFB Gagetown, veterans, military family members and some U.S. lawmakers are still raising concerns these herbicides could linger in the soil. They are calling for more testing as Canada redevelops the base.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Terrorism peace bond imposed on youth who planned school violence in Saskatoon
candidates.description: A 14-year-old who was making plans to carry out a school shooting in Saskatoon will be under a terrorism peace bond for the next year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario's legislature is set for its shortest sitting in decades. Here's what it means for the Ford government
candidates.description: Premier Doug Ford’s government is on pace to sit for the fewest days of any Ontario government over the past five decades in a year when voters aren’t headed to the polls. And experts warn the decision to shorten the calendar at Queen’s Park comes with its strategic benefits — and pitfalls — for the Tories as the legislature settles into a 21-week break.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 2 First Nations say Ontario's 1st Anishinaabe-led impact assessment is done, possibly allowing mining project
candidates.description: Two First Nations in northwestern Ontario are allowing a mining project to move forward after completing what they say is the province’s first Anishinaabe-led impact assessment (ALIA) — but they aren’t supporting or opposing the development. Cat Lake First Nation and Lac Seul First Nation say they’ve authorized the project, but only if First Mining Gold meets 35 negotiated terms.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some artists feeling 'blue dot fever,' fans say, as high ticket prices make concertgoers more selective
candidates.description: The name "blue dot fever" comes from the blue dots on Ticketmaster's website, indicating unsold seats. Some fans say they're going to fewer shows because of the high price tag.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Developer withdraws plans to build northern Ontario AI data centre over public opposition
candidates.description: A developer has withdrawn its plans to build a two-megawatt artificial intelligence (AI) data centre in Armour Township, Ont., due to public opposition to the project.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Federal Court challenge says Canada failing to protect refugees turned back to U.S.
candidates.description: Advocates say Ottawa is ignoring safeguards required by a 2023 Supreme Court ruling, leaving asylum seekers vulnerable to detention and deportation after being denied entry at the border.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hot bet summer? Soon Canadians will be able to trade on prediction markets
candidates.description: Canadian retail investors will be able to access prediction markets through a Canadian company this summer, after the platforms had been banned for years. Prediction markets are experiencing explosive growth, and seen as both a financial vehicle and as gambling.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Vancouver bar nearly drunk dry by FIFA fans as World Cup brings surge in business to Granville Street
candidates.description: Vancouver bars are rushing beer and staff to the front line of the World Cup's war on thirst.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ex-Calgary councillor directed use of personal phone over freedom of information concerns: docs
candidates.description: While investigating allegations of corruption at Calgary city hall, police say they uncovered text messages sent by former councillor Sean Chu to a man now accused of offering money for council votes in which Chu said to “keep conversations to his personal phone” because his work phone was “subject to Freedom of Information” disclosure.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Israeli military publishes new, expanded Lebanon occupation map despite U.S.-Iran deal
candidates.description: Israel's military published a map on Thursday showing an expanded zone of control for its troops in southern Lebanon, as Israeli officials told Reuters Israel was holding talks with the U.S. over continuing its deployment.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canadian killed in South Africa wildlife park
candidates.description: A Canadian tourist has been shot and killed at the Kruger National Park in South Africa, according to officials there.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ukraine hits Moscow oil refinery again, part of massive drone wave inside Russia
candidates.description: Ukraine hit a Moscow oil refinery for a second time in a week and disrupted commercial flights at Moscow airports in one of its biggest drone attacks since Russia's invasion more than four years ago, Russian officials said Thursday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: IN PHOTOS | Knicks championship celebration
candidates.description: New Yorkers held a ticker-tape parade and victory rally on Thursday for the Knicks men's basketball team that brought home the NBA championship after a 53-year drought.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Church of England apologizes for its role in decades of forced adoptions
candidates.description: The Church of England apologized Thursday for its role in forced adoptions as recent as the mid-1970s, acknowledging the painful experiences of many unmarried women at so-called mother and baby homes in the United Kingdom that were affiliated with the church.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Leave it to the beavers: How the industrious mammals are helping to stop a London tube station from flooding
candidates.description: A family of beavers that came to London in 2023 is thriving in west London, and preventing flooding by doing what they do best — building dams.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: NATO wants 'credible' spending plans from members at next month's summit
candidates.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte says member nations must arrive at next month's summit with credible plans to meet the alliance's new five per cent defence spending benchmark. The demand intensifies pressure on Canada, which has faced sharp criticism from Pentagon officials over the absence of a detailed roadmap for future military spending.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Detroit automakers, union set to open bargaining against backdrop of tariffs, CUSMA review
candidates.description: Unifor, which typically uses pattern bargaining for its auto sector negotiations, will go toe-to-toe with Ford first , just as it did three years ago. Talks with Stellantis and GM are expected to follow.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran sign deal that includes plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz, 2-month window for nuclear talks
candidates.description: Senior U.S. officials dictated the memorandum of understanding with Iran to journalists Wednesday after days of secrecy, and Iran suggested that its deal with the United States could be signed by U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Teen dies after being thrown from horse-drawn carriage in New York's Central Park
candidates.description: A teenager thrown to the ground Wednesday when a Central Park carriage horse bolted away from its driver has died, according to police.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: More than 1,000 Palestinians killed since Israel-Hamas ceasefire, says Gaza Health Ministry
candidates.description: Israeli operations in the Gaza Strip have killed 1,005 Palestinians since Israel and Hamas reached a ceasefire last October, the Gaza Health Ministry said Wednesday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fact check: Is this Iranian soccer player real or AI?
candidates.description: This image circulated during the Iran-New Zealand FIFA World Cup match, but is it real? CBC’s Fact Check team breaks down how to tell.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Federal Reserve leaves interest rate unchanged, but some policymakers expect hike this year
candidates.description: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Wednesday, but policymakers ‌expect a hike in borrowing costs later this year amid growing concerns about inflation lodged above the U.S. central bank's two per cent target.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: G7 backs Canada as major global energy supplier to lessen reliance on Strait of Hormuz
candidates.description: Canada is poised to become a key and reliable supplier of energy to the G7 after leaders meeting in France embraced this country's potential to deliver "significant additional capacity" to global markets to reduce dependence on oil and gas coming through the Strait of Hormuz.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Vancouver Goldeneyes pick American defender Caroline Harvey with 1st pick of 2026 PWHL Draft
candidates.description: Generational American defender Caroline Harvey is a member of the Vancouver Goldeneyes. The 23-year-old was selected first overall by Vancouver at the 2026 PWHL Draft in Detroit on Wednesday night.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Matchmaking and cash: South Korea's plan to avoid population decline
candidates.description: Canada also faces a record-low fertility rate, and experts are looking to South Korea to see what they're doing right after years of plummeting birthrates.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump says he'll delay nomination for Clayton to be his next intel chief
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday derailed the confirmation process of his own nominee to head the nation's intelligence agencies, an extraordinary move that upended Senate efforts to renew a crucial surveillance program that expired last week and fuelled fresh tensions with fellow Republicans on Capitol Hill.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Rex Heuermann, Long Island-based serial killer, sentenced to life in prison
candidates.description: Rex Heuermann, a New York architect who murdered women for at least 17 years until police realized their deaths were the work of a serial killer, was sentenced Wednesday to life in prison without parole.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Stevie Wonder, Bruce Springsteen among star-studded list performing at Obama centre's opening
candidates.description: Christina Aguilera, Stevie Wonder, Jennifer Hudson, John Legend, U2's Bono and the Edge, Eddie Vedder and Bruce Springsteen are among the musicians scheduled to take the stage celebrating the opening of the Obama Presidential Center’s Grand Opening Ceremony in Chicago's South Side.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How is Donald Trump's MAGA coalition feeling ahead of midterm elections?
candidates.description: Ahead of the midterm elections in November, some of U.S. President Donald Trump's MAGA base is turning away. Some are citing the war in Iran, while others are growing frustrated with the cost of living in the United States.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's population drops in first quarter of 2026
candidates.description: Canada's population dipped by about 55,000 people in the first three months of this year, according to new numbers from Statistics Canada, which estimates the population as of April 1 was 41,417,056.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Their goal was: use as much AI as possible. Now some companies are pulling back
candidates.description: Prominent tech businesses pushing to use AI intensively have been stung by sky high AI costs, especially for agentic chain-of-thought purposes. Now, they're looking at getting beyond experimentation to tokenomics: really breaking down the return on investment for big AI spending.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Competition Bureau to examine Canada's food supply chain
candidates.description: The Competition Bureau is looking to investigate how competition along the food supply chain affects grocery prices for Canadian consumers.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Rogers, Bell, Telus under fire for charging fees that 'appear' to violate new rules
candidates.description: Rogers, Bell and Telus are each facing scrutiny from Canada's telecom regulator for introducing wireless fees the regulator says appear to violate new federal rules.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: May home sales down 5.1%, but CREA sees positive momentum
candidates.description: Despite year-over-year drop, activity was up 5.5 per cent on a seasonally adjusted basis compared with April this year. Prices have also begun to stabilize as buyers and sellers increasingly see eye to eye, according to CREA's senior economist
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What it will take to restore global energy flow — and bring down gas prices
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says "let the oil flow." But actually getting global oil markets back to anything close to normal will take time, money and pose an immense logistical challenge.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Bell Canada parent company slashing nearly 700 jobs as part of ongoing reorganization
candidates.description: Bell Canada's parent company, BCE, has confirmed it is cutting another 690 jobs as part of a reorganization effort that began late last year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Global stocks soar and oil prices drop as U.S., Iran reach tentative deal to end war
candidates.description: Oil prices fell more than $4 US a barrel on news of a deal to end the fighting, as world markets responded positively to the developments. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq Composite all rose in early trading on Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fox agrees to buy streaming pioneer Roku for $22B US
candidates.description: The deal gives Fox access to more than 100 million households already using Roku, and will help the traditional TV giant push further into the world of streaming.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with U.S. directive
candidates.description: AI giant Anthropic said on Friday it has taken its latest artificial intelligence models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent their use by foreign nationals.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ottawa has struggled to increase grocery competition. Will the new food strategy help?
candidates.description: The federal government has pledged $3 billion which it says will add more food terminals in Canada, help the Competition Bureau sniff out and penalize anticompetitive behaviour, and help Canada produce more of its own food. Independent grocers say it's a step in the right direction when it comes to helping them compete, but won't topple the dominance of the country's major grocers.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: SpaceX stock jumps after record IPO, making Musk the first trillionaire
candidates.description: SpaceX soared in its Nasdaq debut on Friday, making the company worth more than $2 trillion US. It's expected to be included in the Nasdaq 100 within weeks, making it a major holding for passive funds and ETFs that track the index.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: SpaceX kicks off wave of monster IPOs: What to know about companies going public
candidates.description: IPOs like SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI have dominated headlines lately, as a number of high-profile private companies announced they will become publicly traded firms. The hype is hard to ignore — but could these IPOs fall flat?
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Dealership revoked offer to buy back customer's BMW, blaming wayward AI chatbot
candidates.description: A Toronto man says he was shocked and devastated when a BMW dealership revoked an offer to buy back his car, telling him an AI chatbot generated the offer by mistake. The dealership has since reinstated the deal, but as Canadian businesses rush to adopt artificial intelligence tools, they face a growing risk of customer backlash if those tools make mistakes.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
candidates.description: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why Iran believes deal with US leaves it stronger than before
candidates.description: Iran has come out of the war's first chapter stronger than many expected, but its next challenge may be harder, the BBC Persian Service's Amir Azimi writes.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
candidates.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
candidates.description: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
candidates.description: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
candidates.description: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
candidates.description: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
candidates.description: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
candidates.description: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Teenager dies in horse-drawn carriage accident in New York
candidates.description: The horse bolted and toppled over after the carriage driver stepped down to take a picture of the passengers.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
candidates.description: In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The Ring and Lilo & Stitch actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
candidates.description: Chase died from sepsis following a bout with meningitis in a Los Angeles hospital, her longtime manager told the BBC.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
candidates.description: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bystanders smash windows of private jet to rescue passengers after crash
candidates.description: One of the six people on board died in the crash, a local official said. The five survivors were taken to hospital and are in a stable condition.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: AI will create more jobs for humans, not replace them, Amazon founder Bezos says
candidates.description: The Amazon founder, who now has robotics and space travel companies, thinks AI will create a labour shortage.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Group planned to attack White House UFC event using snipers and drones, FBI says
candidates.description: In newly unsealed court filings, the government says the group allegedly expressed grievances about corruption, the Epstein files, and data centres.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
candidates.description: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
candidates.description: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
candidates.description: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
candidates.description: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
candidates.description: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
candidates.description: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Five big questions about the UK's under-16s social media ban
candidates.description: A ban is coming - but it's still not clear what it will mean for sites including Roblox, YouTube and WhatsApp.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What is Helium-3 and could we get it from the moon?
candidates.description: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet
candidates.description: The BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman on the big changes coming down the line for young people online.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Under-16s will be banned from social media from early 2027
candidates.description: Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Who is Elon Musk and what is his net worth?
candidates.description: The boss of X, Tesla and SpaceX, already the world's richest person, is now also its first trillionaire.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
candidates.description: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Nasa names next astronauts for Artemis Moon programme
candidates.description: Nasa names its next Artemis crew, though they will not be walking on the Moon or even going anywhere near it.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
candidates.description: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
candidates.description: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Next El Niño could be strongest in decades
candidates.description: A new phase of the natural El Niño weather pattern could begin in a matter of weeks, the UN has warned, boosting temperatures on a planet already under strain from climate change.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: New microplastics research examines River Thames
candidates.description: Water samples are assessed for pollution levels and any impact climate pressures may be having on the river system.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
candidates.description: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Elon Musk gets public trading of SpaceX under way from Texas
candidates.description: SpaceX founder Elon Musk said he gave the company "less than a 10% chance of succeeding at all" when it first started, and it is now going public "with the largest IPO ever".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Delhi's temperature showed 43.5C. Why did it feel hotter?
candidates.description: We spent a day out in the city with a thermal camera, recording surface temperatures indoors and outdoors.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What are UV levels and how can you protect yourself?
candidates.description: Some UV exposure is essential for our wellbeing, but too much is damaging and can cause skin cancer.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The 40 minutes when the Artemis crew loses contact with the Earth
candidates.description: As the astronauts pass behind the Moon they will experience a moment of silence and solitude as communication with the Earth is blocked.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
candidates.description: Lunar discoveries and a space race with China is seeing the US invest time and money to get to the Moon - and beyond.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why cheap power could matter more than clean power in the push for net zero
candidates.description: The question of how important making our electricity clean is to going green is coming under increasing scrutiny
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The debate about whether the NHS should use magic mushrooms to treat depression
candidates.description: Many clinical trials to test the use of psychedelic medicines for conditions such as depression have been underway since 2022 - with surprising results
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: COP30: Trump and many leaders are skipping it, so does the summit still have a point?
candidates.description: The US president is notably absent from these UN climate talks, as are other world leaders, all of which prompts questions about the purpose of COP today.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
candidates.description: Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
candidates.description: Ukraine launched a new wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday, amounting to one of the largest attacks on the Russian capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
candidates.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
candidates.description: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Hegseth announces in Brussels a review of U.S. forces in Europe, and a 'NATO 3.0'
candidates.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: For Lebanon, a U.S.-Iran agreement raises questions beyond Hezbollah
candidates.description: Former Lebanese ambassador to Jordan, Tracy Chamoun, explains what the U.S.-Iran agreement means for Lebanon — and what a ceasefire would look like on the ground.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FIFA hydration breaks have sparked criticism. But what do they actually do?
candidates.description: For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Over 1,000 people killed during Gaza ceasefire, Palestinian authorities say
candidates.description: Israeli operations in the Gaza Strip have killed 1,005 Palestinians since a ceasefire was reached between Israel and the militant group Hamas last October, according to Gaza Health Ministry.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Here's how much the Iran war cost -- and how its effects will linger
candidates.description: More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Uzbekistan's first World Cup appearance is a dream come true for players and fans
candidates.description: The World Cup debut of Uzbekistan signals the region's growing confidence on the global stage and, some argue, the arrival of a "golden generation" making its mark in sports, culture and science.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
candidates.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady and hints at rate hike later this year
candidates.description: The Federal Reserve left its benchmark interest rates unchanged Wednesday, and signaled its next move could be a rate increase. It's the first rate decision under the new Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: With Iran deal, Trump told ships to 'start your engines.' That's not happening yet
candidates.description: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz inflicted global pain during the months-long conflict with the U.S. and Israel. A tentative deal is in place, but questions remain about the key waterway.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Shipping exec says industry needs assurances Strait of Hormuz will be safe
candidates.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Jakob Larsen, chief safety and security officer at BIMCO, the global shipping association, about what it might mean to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The war with Iran is making oil changes pricier. And a deal won't solve it
candidates.description: The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Survey confirms the struggle of working parents: 'No way to be two things at once'
candidates.description: A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Is it a renter's market? It depends on where you live
candidates.description: About 40% of rentals on Zillow offer move-in deals, like a month of free rent, thanks to a construction boom that created an apartment surplus in some parts of the United States.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Visa and OpenAI integrate Visa's secure global payment directly into ChatGPT
candidates.description: Would you hand an AI chatbot your credit card? This week, Visa's deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI became the latest step in the march toward a future where AI offers to shop on your behalf.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: How much of an economic boom is the 2026 FIFA World Cup for the U.S. hosting cities?
candidates.description: The FIFA World Cup is thought to bring an economic windfall to the cities and regions where games will be played during four weeks this summer. How true is that?
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: You're probably using too many skin care products. Here are the 3 essentials
candidates.description: We asked half a dozen skin care experts: Which products do you really need to keep your skin healthy and attractive? Here's what they said.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These 3 brothers lost their parents to AIDS. Now they struggle to make it on their own
candidates.description: Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: As America turns 250, one museum makes history possible to touch
candidates.description: Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pakistan ends 'luxury tax' on menstrual products, contraceptives. Will prices drop?
candidates.description: In Pakistan, taxes on menstrual products can add up. Activists have long worked to change this. Now a new budget wipes out the 18% sales tax. But questions remain about the impact on prices.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Winning strategy to prevent diabetes and related chronic diseases
candidates.description: In the U.S., 115 million adults have prediabetes, which is tied to heart disease and other health problems. A 20-year follow-up to a landmark diabetes study shows lifestyle interventions cut the risk.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump further guts Education Dept. by shifting oversight of special ed, civil rights
candidates.description: The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Live with a partner? You may be sharing more microbes than you think
candidates.description: A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Is a transparent fish the future of brain science? This center is betting on it
candidates.description: One of the world's leading brain research centers is shifting away from fruit flies and toward a tiny, transparent fish. The goal: to understand how brains control the behavior of an animal or human.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Ticks surge in the Northeast
candidates.description: It's prime tick season, especially in the Northeast and Midwest. The prevalence of ticks is changing the way some people in highly affected areas live their lives.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Most parents track their 18- to 25-year-old kids on their smartphones. Is it healthy?
candidates.description: A new survey from the University of Michigan asks parents about their use of technology to track their adult children, ages 18-25, including using "always on" location tracking on their smartphones.
candidates.author: NPR
